meta.title: AWS-DEA-4: Ingestion, Warehousing, and Governance on AWS

questions.stem: A data team needs to capture application events at high throughput and allow multiple consumers to process the same ordered stream independently. Which service is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because the official documentation supports that choice for the stated scenario. The remaining options do not directly meet the requirement.

[Official reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/streams/latest/dev/introduction.html)

questions.stem: An organization wants to ingest database changes from Amazon RDS into Amazon S3 with minimal custom code and continuous CDC. Which service should it use?
questions.explanation:
**D** is correct because the official documentation supports that choice for the stated scenario. The remaining options do not directly meet the requirement.

[Official reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)

questions.stem: A streaming pipeline must enrich records with a custom Lambda function and then deliver the results to Amazon S3 and Amazon Redshift with managed buffering and retry behavior. Which service is most appropriate?

options.text: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink

**B** is correct because the official documentation supports that choice for the stated scenario. The remaining options do not directly meet the requirement.

[Official reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/firehose/latest/dev/what-is-this-service.html)

questions.stem: An AWS Glue batch job should process only new files added since the previous successful run. Which feature directly supports this pattern?

questions.stem: An engineer is designing a near-real-time analytics pipeline on AWS. Which TWO statements are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink is suitable for stateful stream processing.
Amazon Data Firehose is mainly designed as a managed delivery service, not a general-purpose stream processor.
AWS Glue crawlers are the preferred engine for sub-second event processing.
Amazon S3 event notifications provide ordered replayable streams for many independent consumers.

questions.explanation:
**A and B** are correct because the official documentation supports that choice for the stated scenario. The remaining options do not directly meet the requirement.

[Official reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managed-flink/latest/java/what-is.html)

questions.stem: A Glue ETL job writes hundreds of thousands of tiny Parquet files to S3. What is the best way to reduce file counts during the write?

**B** is correct because the official documentation supports that choice for the stated scenario. The remaining options do not directly meet the requirement.

[Official reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/what-is-glue.html)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO benefits does AWS Glue Schema Registry provide for streaming data? (Select TWO)

options.text: It helps producers and consumers agree on schema versions.
It can enforce schema compatibility rules.

questions.explanation:

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements about the AWS Glue Data Catalog are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Athena can use catalog table definitions to query S3 data.
It stores all source data rows directly inside the catalog.

questions.stem: An organization wants to retain a historical raw zone and a curated analytics zone in S3 with different prefixes, lifecycle rules, and access policies. What is the strongest reason to separate them logically?

options.text: It allows independent governance, retention, and access controls by zone
It prevents Athena from reading Parquet
It is required because S3 buckets can only store one file format

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because the official documentation supports that choice for the stated scenario. The remaining options do not directly meet the requirement.

[Official reference](https://aws.amazon.com/certification/certified-data-engineer-associate/)
